prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(enqueue_release_unit main_debit_leg)
(enqueue_release_unit main_credit_leg)
(enqueue_release_unit release_workflow_a)
(enqueue_release_unit release_workflow_b)
(allocate_processing_slot main_debit_leg processing_slot_a)
(allocate_processing_slot main_credit_leg processing_slot_b)
(claim_validation_agent main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(confirm_validation_clearance main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(reserve_debit_route_target main_debit_leg debit_route_target_a validation_agent_a)
(release_validation_agent main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(claim_operator_agent main_debit_leg operator_agent_a)
(lock_debit_route_assignment main_debit_leg debit_route_target_a operator_agent_a)
(release_operator_agent main_debit_leg operator_agent_a)
(claim_validation_agent main_credit_leg validation_agent_a)
(confirm_validation_clearance main_credit_leg validation_agent_a)
(reserve_credit_route_target main_credit_leg credit_route_target_a validation_agent_a)
(release_validation_agent main_credit_leg validation_agent_a)
(claim_operator_agent main_credit_leg operator_agent_a)
(lock_credit_route_assignment main_credit_leg credit_route_target_a operator_agent_a)
(release_operator_agent main_credit_leg operator_agent_a)
(assemble_balanced_settlement_message main_debit_leg main_credit_leg debit_route_target_a credit_route_target_a settlement_message_a)
(claim_validation_agent main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(endorse_settlement_message settlement_message_a main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(release_validation_agent main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(prepare_debit_leg_settlement main_debit_leg settlement_message_a)
(prepare_credit_leg_settlement main_credit_leg settlement_message_a)
(allocate_processing_slot release_workflow_a processing_slot_c)
(claim_validation_agent release_workflow_a validation_agent_a)
(confirm_validation_clearance release_workflow_a validation_agent_a)
(claim_signature_token release_workflow_a signature_token_a)
(claim_compliance_verification release_workflow_a compliance_verification_a)
(bind_document_bundle_to_settlement_message release_workflow_a document_bundle_a settlement_message_a)
(stage_release_workflow release_workflow_a document_bundle_a settlement_message_a validation_agent_a)
(claim_operator_agent release_workflow_a operator_agent_a)
(sign_off_release_workflow release_workflow_a signature_token_a operator_agent_a document_bundle_a settlement_message_a)
(release_operator_agent release_workflow_a operator_agent_a)
(release_signature_token release_workflow_a signature_token_a)
(release_compliance_verification release_workflow_a compliance_verification_a)
(authorize_release_workflow release_workflow_a compliance_verification_a document_bundle_a settlement_message_a)
(finalize_standard_release release_workflow_a)
(attach_release_context release_workflow_a release_context_a validation_agent_a)
(release_validation_agent release_workflow_a validation_agent_a)
(complete_release_workflow_settlement release_workflow_a processing_slot_c release_context_a)
(allocate_processing_slot release_workflow_b processing_slot_c)
(claim_validation_agent release_workflow_b validation_agent_a)
(confirm_validation_clearance release_workflow_b validation_agent_a)
(claim_signature_token release_workflow_b signature_token_a)
(claim_compliance_verification release_workflow_b compliance_verification_a)
(bind_document_bundle_to_settlement_message release_workflow_b document_bundle_b settlement_message_a)
(stage_release_workflow release_workflow_b document_bundle_b settlement_message_a validation_agent_a)
(claim_operator_agent release_workflow_b operator_agent_a)
(sign_off_release_workflow release_workflow_b signature_token_a operator_agent_a document_bundle_b settlement_message_a)
(release_operator_agent release_workflow_b operator_agent_a)
(release_signature_token release_workflow_b signature_token_a)
(release_compliance_verification release_workflow_b compliance_verification_a)
(authorize_release_workflow release_workflow_b compliance_verification_a document_bundle_b settlement_message_a)
(finalize_standard_release release_workflow_b)
(attach_release_context release_workflow_b release_context_a validation_agent_a)
(release_validation_agent release_workflow_b validation_agent_a)
(complete_release_workflow_settlement release_workflow_b processing_slot_c release_context_a)
(claim_validation_agent main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(attach_release_context main_debit_leg release_context_a validation_agent_a)
(release_validation_agent main_debit_leg validation_agent_a)
(complete_debit_leg_settlement main_debit_leg processing_slot_a release_context_a)
(claim_validation_agent main_credit_leg validation_agent_a)
(attach_release_context main_credit_leg release_context_a validation_agent_a)
(release_validation_agent main_credit_leg validation_agent_a)
(complete_credit_leg_settlement main_credit_leg processing_slot_b release_context_a)
```

message.reasoning:
The problem involves a payment release queue prioritization domain in PDDL. I need to find a plan that achieves the goal: settlement_completed for main_debit_leg, main_credit_leg, release_workflow_a, and release_workflow_b.


## Examining PDDL Domain  
- The problem focuses on a payment release queue prioritization using PDDL, a planning language.  
- The goal is to achieve settlement completion for main_debit_leg, main_credit_leg, release_workflow_a, and release_workflow_b.

## Examining Workflow Details  
- Both workflows, release_workflow_a and release_workflow_b, link to the same main_debit_leg, main_credit_leg, and settlement_message_a, sharing resources.  
- Document bundles differ, with document_bundle_a for workflow_a and document_bundle_b for workflow_b, affecting individual processing.  

## Checking Availability  
- Priority profiles a and b, exception profiles a and b, release context a, and signature token a are available for processing.  
- External_approval_a is available initially and linked to workflow_a, but external_approval_b is not available, impacting workflow_b processing.  

## Analyzing Release Units  
- Main_debit_leg, main_credit_leg, release_workflow_a, and release_workflow_b are all classified as release_unit, needing to be enqueued for processing.  
- Achieving settlement_completed requires actions like complete_debit_leg_settlement, which depend on execution_committed and other prerequisites like slot_assignment.  

## Exploring Settlement Process  
- Execution_ready is needed before execution_committed, achieved for legs via prepare_debit_leg_settlement requiring debit_leg_locked, cleared, and settlement_message endorsements.  
- For workflows, execution_ready comes from finalize_standard_release or similar, requiring workflow_authorized and no exceptions, complicating shared resource management.

## Exploring Workflow Paths  
- For workflows, execution_ready can be achieved via stage_release_workflow, sign_off, authorize, and finalize_standard_release, avoiding external_approval requirements.  
- This path includes validation_cleared, document bundle linking, and signature token claims, setting staged, signed_off, authorized, and finalized states.  

## Checking External Approval Issues  
- Workflow_b is blocked from recording external_approval due to unavailable external_approval_b, with no action to make it available.  
- This blocks the operator_routed and compliance_cleared path for workflow_b, but standard paths without external_approval remain viable.

## Examining Agent Assignments  
- Validation agents must be assigned to specific debit or credit legs for actions like reserving routes, requiring validation_cleared status first.  
- Since only one validation agent is available, it needs to be assigned to one leg, process actions, and then released for the other leg to proceed.  

## Analyzing Locking Mechanisms  
- Locking debit routes requires operator agent assignment to the leg, route assignment, and validation_cleared status, setting locked and debit_leg_cleared states.  
- Debit_leg_cleared differs from validation_cleared, indicating a distinct processing step after locking.  

## Exploring Workflow Agent Needs  
- Claiming signature tokens or compliance verification for workflows requires validation_cleared status, with no need for validation agent assignment during these steps.  
- Stage_release_workflow, however, does require validation_agent_assignment, while binding document bundles only needs validation_cleared status.  

## Planning Processing Order  
- To minimize conflicts with limited agents, process debit and credit legs fully first, including locking, clearing, and endorsing, before handling workflows.  
- Endorsing settlement messages requires validation agent assignment to the specific leg, even after composing, complicating shared agent management.

## Extending Processing Order  
- Binding document bundles to settlement messages can happen after validation_cleared status, without needing agent assignment, and sets the bundle as not available.  
- For workflows, stage_release_workflow requires validation_agent_assignment after binding, suggesting binding should precede staging to leverage the assignment.  

## Sequencing Workflow Steps  
- Claiming signature tokens and compliance verification can occur early after validation_cleared, without requiring agent assignment, streamlining the process.  
- Sign-off needs staged status, signature link, and operator assignment, which can be claimed after legs are processed, ensuring operator availability.  

## Managing Shared Resources  
- Only one signature token and compliance verification are available, requiring workflows to be processed sequentially to completion before moving to the next.  
- Attaching context to workflows or legs for completion requires committed status and a shared context, necessitating sequential processing due to limited availability.

## Allocating Shared Resources  
- Slot_c is available and can be allocated to wf_a, but not wf_b yet, requiring wf_a to be processed first.  
- Enqueue wf_a and allocate slot_c to it, claiming validation agent val_a and confirming validation_cleared status for wf_a.  

## Binding and Staging Workflow  
- Bind doc_bundle_a to msg_a for wf_a after validation_cleared, setting the bundle as bound and linked.  
- Stage wf_a using bundle_a, msg_a, and val_a, requiring doc_bound, msg_link, and no debit exceptions, setting staged status.  

## Signing Off and Authorizing  
- Claim operator agent op_a for wf_a after validation_cleared, then sign_off using sig_a, op_a, bundle_a, and msg_a, setting signed_off.  
- Authorize wf_a with comp_a, bundle_a, and msg_a after sign_off, setting authorized due to no exceptions flagged.  

## Finalizing and Completing Workflow  
- Finalize wf_a to set execution_ready, then attach release_context_a to wf_a using validation_agent_a, setting committed and linking context.  
- Complete wf_a settlement with slot_c and release_context_a, freeing slot_c and context, making them available for wf_b.  

## Managing Validation Agent Conflicts  
- After completing wf_a, val_a remains assigned, preventing allocation to wf_b without releasing it first.  
- Releasing val_a from wf_a requires specific preconditions, ensuring sequential processing to manage shared resources.

## Handling Release Workflow for wf_b  
- Release operator_agent_a and signature_token_a for release_workflow_b after sign-off, ensuring resources are freed.  
- Authorize release_workflow_b using compliance_verification_a, document_bundle_b, and settlement_message_a, setting authorized status.  

## Finalizing and Completing wf_b  
- Finalize release_workflow_b and attach release_context_a using validation_agent_a, setting committed status and linking context.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
